objective: Map the Phase-1 P(drift_reached_bid) predictor to bid-offset aggressiveness (per-event tiered offset: larger when P high, smaller/safer when P low), layered on the Path B v3 per-regime table. Tests whether feature-conditioned offset MODULATION beats v3 place-everywhere - the channel Phase 1 recommended after binary skip-gating failed (A42).

sources.path_b_v3_per_n_simulation.parquet: v3 per-N realized PnL baseline for the comparison
sources.per_regime_offsets_v1.csv: v3 baseline per-regime offsets (the offsets being modulated)
sources.phase1_holdout_model: 3-feature logistic, holdout AUC 0.7298 - P(drift) applied to all N

method: Four P-threshold parameterizations splitting the corpus into aggressive/neutral/conservative tiers, each tier applying a tier-specific offset on top of v3. Gross ROI (pre-fee) vs v3 repro. P from the holdout-trained model applied to all N (in-sample-optimistic for CPCV rows - the negative result strengthens out of sample).

headline.verdict: NEGATIVE - best variant +0.089pp gross is noise (inside run-to-run jitter, vanishes pre-realism); conditional offset does not beat v3 in 14/36 cells.

structural_insight: Offset modulation reshuffles WHICH events fill and at WHAT discount, but the entry discount only changes hold-to-settlement minority payoffs; on the majority triggered-exit outcomes realized PnL is +X regardless of entry (atlas fixed-profit exit cap). Confidence-weighting the offset cannot move the payoff that dominates the blend. See path_c_arc_findings.md Section 3/6 and LESSONS A43.

validation_gates.gate6_material_roi: NOT CLEARED (documented negative, not a STOP) - best +0.089pp gross < 0.5pp material bar and far below v3's 12.11%; pre-realism it is zero. Offset modulation is not a productive lever.

recommendation: DO NOT promote to policy. The predictor is real (Phase 1 AUC 0.73) but the atlas fixed-profit exit caps entry-side conditioning. The productive lever is the STATIC per-cell offset re-optimized on net-PnL (Path B v4 T47, +1.024pp), not per-event modulation.
deviations: Gross (pre-fee) comparison - the modulation gain is pre-fee; net it is no better.
P from holdout-trained model applied to all N (in-sample-optimistic for CPCV rows); negative result is robust to the optimism.
